mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust and expanding labor market, which typically translates into higher consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with PAYEMS month-over-month growth (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and broader economic contraction, leading to investor pessimism and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) show strong growth while the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains low, it indicates robust consumer demand supported by accommodative monetary policy, fostering a positive environment for corporate earnings and equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 30% of periods where RSAFS growth is strong and FEDFUNDS is low.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated inflation, a sustained rise in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) suggests eroding purchasing power and the potential for aggressive monetary tightening, which negatively impacts corporate valuations and S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL shows a 12-month change exceeding 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) signals robust consumer and business confidence in the economy's future, stimulating construction and related industries, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of periods following a strong HOUST signal.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates a weakening manufacturing sector and broader economic slowdown, which typically translates into lower corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 35% of instances where INDPRO shows a significant decline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is low and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is rising, it suggests the central bank is actively tightening monetary policy to curb inflation, which typically dampens economic growth and equity market performance over a quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25pp in 3 months AND UNRATE < 6.0%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where FEDFUNDS is rising and UNRATE is low.

mechanism_narrative: Robust real GDP growth (GDPC1) reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which generally supports corporate profitability, investor confidence, and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are not positively correlated with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth (Rank IC < 0.05) over any 10-year rolling window.
